expectations.text: Mentions KaTeX as the validation library
expectations.evidence: Response recommends KaTeX explicitly and provides the validate-math.mjs script.

expectations.text: Provides an install command (bun add -g katex or npm install -g katex)
expectations.evidence: Response provides 'bun add -g katex' as the install command.

expectations.text: Provides or references a Node.js/JavaScript script that validates all three block types
expectations.evidence: Response references the bundled validate-math.mjs script handling all three block types.

expectations.text: Mentions that the solution exits with code 1 on errors (CI-friendly)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'exits with code 1 if any errors (CI-friendly)'.

expectations.text: Notes the important caveat that KaTeX passing does NOT guarantee GitHub renders correctly
expectations.evidence: Response has a dedicated 'Critical caveat' section about GitHub pre-processor stripping \\ inside $$ blocks.

expectations.text: Identifies the root cause as GitHub's Markdown pre-processor stripping \\ before the math renderer
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly names 'GitHub's Markdown pre-processor runs before the math renderer' and explains it collapses \\ to \.

expectations.text: Recommends converting to ```math fenced block syntax
expectations.evidence: Response directly recommends replacing $$ with ```math fenced block as the canonical fix.

expectations.text: Provides a corrected code example using ```math...``` instead of $$...$$
expectations.evidence: Response provides the corrected matrix equation using ```math fenced block syntax.

expectations.text: Generalizes the rule to all equations containing \\ (not just matrices)
expectations.evidence: Response states the rule applies to 'any multi-line equation containing \\'.

expectations.text: Identifies the PDF as Type A (Word-generated) based on the Unicode math copy-paste clue
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly identifies the Unicode math symbols (∑, π, σ) as 'the definitive sign of a Type A Word-generated PDF' and leads with this diagnosis.

expectations.text: Recommends pymupdf4llm as the prose extraction tool
expectations.evidence: Response recommends pymupdf4llm with the uv run command.

expectations.text: Explicitly warns that marker-pdf will NOT work on this PDF type
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'Never use marker-pdf for this PDF type' and explains it fails silently.

expectations.text: States that math equations must be manually transcribed from PDF screenshots
expectations.evidence: Response states 'there is no shortcut' and provides 3-step manual transcription process.

expectations.text: Mentions the $$ vs ```math distinction or multi-line rendering rule
expectations.evidence: Response covers the $$-vs-```math decision including the pre-processor stripping issue.

expectations.text: Mentions KaTeX as the validation library
expectations.evidence: Response recommends KaTeX as the core validation library.

expectations.text: Provides an install command (bun add -g katex or npm install -g katex)
expectations.evidence: Response provides npm install katex instructions.

expectations.text: Provides or references a Node.js/JavaScript script that validates all three block types

expectations.text: Mentions that the solution exits with code 1 on errors (CI-friendly)
expectations.evidence: Response mentions 'exits with code 1' and provides a GitHub Actions workflow example.

expectations.text: Notes the important caveat that KaTeX passing does NOT guarantee GitHub renders correctly
expectations.evidence: Response never mentions the GitHub pre-processor issue. Users will be confused when equations validate locally but fail on GitHub.

expectations.text: Identifies the root cause as GitHub's Markdown pre-processor stripping \\ before the math renderer
expectations.evidence: Response says 'parser consumes backslashes' — partially correct but misses the pre-processor architecture. Primary fix recommended is \\\\ doubling which is WRONG.

expectations.text: Recommends converting to ```math fenced block syntax
expectations.evidence: Primary recommendation is \\\\ (quadruple backslash/doubling) — incorrect for GitHub GFM.

expectations.text: Provides a corrected code example using ```math...``` instead of $$...$$
expectations.evidence: Provides code with \\ doubling approach which does not render correctly in GitHub GFM.

expectations.text: Generalizes the rule to all equations containing \\ (not just matrices)
expectations.evidence: Response mentions this affects matrix/array environments generally.

expectations.text: Identifies the PDF as Type A (Word-generated) based on the Unicode math copy-paste clue
expectations.evidence: Response does not identify PDF type. Treats marker-pdf as the universal solution.

expectations.text: Recommends pymupdf4llm as the prose extraction tool
expectations.evidence: Response recommends marker-pdf as primary tool, not pymupdf4llm.

expectations.text: Explicitly warns that marker-pdf will NOT work on this PDF type
expectations.evidence: Response ACTIVELY RECOMMENDS marker-pdf — the exact anti-pattern. No warning.

expectations.text: States that math equations must be manually transcribed from PDF screenshots
expectations.evidence: Response assumes marker-pdf will extract LaTeX automatically.

expectations.evidence: Response mentions using $$ for display equations, though not the multi-line rule.
